description:
## Problem

Both places that tell a user/agent about the missing-ADR condition are one-armed: they name "write an ADR" as the only action, so the escape hatch that already exists (`Spec-Drift:` trailer, BranchCheck.Trailer) is undiscoverable at the moment of decision.

1. `lib/specled_ex/next.ex:187` — `needs_decision_update` step reads "Add or revise an ADR if this branch changes durable cross-cutting policy." The conditional has no else-action: an agent that judges the change NOT durable/cross-cutting has no named way to clear the `needs decision update` verdict, so it writes the ADR anyway.
2. `lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:92-97` — `branch_guard_missing_decision_update` emits "Cross-cutting change spans multiple subjects but no decision file changed" with a nil fix block, unlike the append_only/* findings which end with a code-fenced `fix:` block.

The trailer is documented only in the skill's escape-hatches section, far from both decision points. Downstream evidence (Atlas repo): 210 decision files in ~8 weeks (~2.5/day), roughly a third commit-grade (module renames, form-state fixes, temporary shims) — exactly what decisions/README.md says does not belong. Agents obey the router's literal text; the one-armed sentence is the mechanism producing filler ADRs. Atlas's workaround was demoting the code to :warning repo-wide (after the atlas-vmi hard-fail), which loses the gate entirely while agents still comply with the warning — worst of both.

## Proposed fix

Make both messages carry the fork explicitly, rubric first, ADR as the affirmative arm:

- `next.ex` `needs_decision_update` steps become two arms:
  - "If this branch changes durable cross-cutting policy, add or revise an ADR (`mix spec.decision.new <id> --title ...`)."
  - "If it does not, record `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info` as a git trailer on a commit in this range, with a one-line reason in the commit body."
- `branch_check.ex` gives the finding a `fix:` block with the same two arms, matching the append_only/* message convention.
- Skill template (`skills/` + `mix spec.init` scaffold): add the fork to the ADR section so generated local skills say when the trailer is the sanctioned answer to this finding, not just an escape hatch for drift findings.

## Design caution

Advertising a silencing mechanism in the main loop risks reflexive use. Mitigate by ordering/framing: state the rubric (durable? cross-cutting? constrains future changes? records a rejected alternative?) before either arm, keep the ADR as the affirmative path, and require the trailer to carry a reason. Net property vs config demotion: trailers are per-range and visible in commit history/review, so each "no ADR needed" call is individually auditable instead of policy-wide and silent.

## Acceptance

- `mix spec.next` output for `needs_decision_update` names both arms including the exact trailer syntax.
- `mix spec.check` `branch_guard_missing_decision_update` finding message ends with a `fix:` block naming both arms.
- Generated skill/docs mention the fork where the ADR obligation is described.
- Existing trailer semantics unchanged (range-wide, `trailer > config > default`, config :off absorbing).

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-4kg-advise-the-spec-drift
Log: interactive session 68b444f8-0674-4f46-82e3-7b54e6fd6181

Commit: 22c7ece
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/next.ex, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, lib/specled_ex/prime.ex, priv/spec_init/README.md.eex, priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex, .spec/README.md, .spec/decisions/specled.decision.decision_fork_advertised_at_decision_points.md, .spec/specs/{next,branch_guard,prime,mix_tasks,package,severity,append_only,implementation_tier,spec_drift_trailer}.spec.md, test/mix/tasks/spec_next_task_test.exs, test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
Tests added: Mix.Tasks.SpecNextTaskTest "spec.next names both ADR and trailer arms when only the decision is missing" (@tag spec: specled.next.decision_fork_guidance); SpecLedEx.BranchCheckTest "ends with a fix block naming the ADR and trailer arms" (@tag spec: specled.branch_guard.missing_decision_fix_block)
Verification stubs flipped: none — new requirement ids specled.next.decision_fork_guidance and specled.branch_guard.missing_decision_fix_block added to existing execute:true tagged_tests blocks
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test --stale (1019 tests), plus targeted branch_check_test.exs, spec_next_task_test.exs, severity_integration_test.exs, spec_tasks_test.exs, spec_prime_task_test.exs, trailer_test.exs (92 tests); mix spec.check --no-run-commands --base origin/main = 0 findings; mix format --check-formatted clean; full-system proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: Both missing-ADR surfaces now present the durable-policy rubric and both resolution arms: spec.next needs_decision_update steps name the ADR command and the exact Spec-Drift trailer syntax with a required one-line reason, and branch_guard_missing_decision_update ends with a code-fenced fix: block matching the append_only convention. The spec.prime loop line, spec.init scaffold README + local skill (incl. a new triage-table row), and .spec/README.md carry the same fork, and ADR specled.decision.decision_fork_advertised_at_decision_points records the policy (per-range trailers preferred over config demotion). Trailer semantics untouched — range-wide, trailer > config > default, :off absorbing.

title: Advise the Spec-Drift trailer alternative in spec.next and the missing_decision_update finding
